paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VI. Banana trees
summary: No follow-up paper resolving this open question was found in the indexed literature. The question asks which multigraphs H are 'pervasive' in the weak sense that H-subdivision-free graphs with bounded clique number have bounded chromatic number; banana trees are known to have this property (the main result of arXiv:1701.05597), but a full characterisation remains elusive. The Scott–Seymour chi-boundedness survey (arXiv:1812.07500) covers related material but its full text was inaccessible for verification. The two supplied internal references were verified and are unrelated to this question.

internal_refs_verified.note: Paper is about tournament structure (whether large-chromatic-number tournaments contain vertex-disjoint subtournaments with directed edges between them); it does not address H-subdivision-free graphs or the pervasiveness question. Supplied contribution text is incorrect for this conjecture — likely a cross-match artefact.

Paper is the fourth instalment of the 'Asymptotic structure' series and provides a counterexample to the weak coarse Menger conjecture; it does not discuss H-subdivision-free graphs, clique number, or chromatic number pervasiveness. Supplied contribution text is incorrect for this conjecture — likely a cross-match artefact.

notes: No follow-up resolution found after 3 web searches and 2 WebFetch calls. The arXiv preprint dates to January 2017 (published 2019), making this an 8-year-old open problem; absence of a found resolution with medium confidence. The chi-boundedness survey arXiv:1812.07500 (Scott–Seymour, 2020) is a likely secondary source but its PDF could not be parsed. Both supplied internal references are false positives from the fuzzy-matching pipeline and do not cite or address this conjecture.
